# mdocUI

> Generative UI library for LLMs using Markdoc tag syntax inline with markdown prose

## Overview

mdocUI lets LLMs write natural markdown and drop interactive UI components in the same stream using Markdoc `{% %}` tag syntax. The streaming parser tokenizes chunks character-by-character and the React renderer displays 24 theme-neutral components — charts, buttons, forms, tables, cards, and more.

## Packages

- @mdocui/core: Streaming parser, component registry, system prompt generator
- @mdocui/react: React renderer, 24 theme-neutral components, useRenderer hook
- @mdocui/cli: Project scaffolder, preview server, prompt generator

## Key Concepts

- Markdoc `{% %}` tag syntax for inline UI with prose
- Streaming-first: character-by-character tokenizer handles partial chunks
- Two-layer prompt system: library auto-generates syntax docs, app adds domain context
- Theme-neutral: components use currentColor/inherit, consumers style via classNames
- One-shot interactions: buttons disable after click, forms lock after submit
- Error boundaries: per-component recovery, one failure won't crash the tree

## Syntax

Self-closing tag:
{% button action="continue" label="Click me" /%}

Body tag:
{% card title="Hello" %}
Content here with **markdown** support.
{% /card %}

Nested:
{% card title="Dashboard" %}
{% grid cols=3 %}
{% stat label="Revenue" value="$12K" /%}
{% /grid %}
{% /card %}

## Components (24)

Layout: stack, grid, card, divider, accordion, tabs, tab
Interactive: button, button-group, input, textarea, select, checkbox, toggle, form
Data: chart, table, stat, progress
Content: callout, badge, image, code-block, link

## Links

- Homepage: https://mdocui.vercel.app
- Documentation: https://mdocui.github.io
